mission.title: linkerd: Make Prometheus Pluggable
mission.description: Make Prometheus Pluggable. Requested by 5+ users.

steps.title: Check current linkerd deployment
steps.description:
Verify your linkerd version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n linkerd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=linkerd
helm list -n linkerd 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working linkerd installation.

steps.title: Review linkerd configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant linkerd configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n linkerd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=linkerd
kubectl get configmap -n linkerd -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=linkerd
```
## Feature Request

The prometheus instance that comes with Linkerd is nice but for large clusters this can become a bottleneck.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Make Prometheus Pluggable
steps.description:
For a bit more context, here are some docs around integration `linkerd-prometheus` metrics with other tools:
https://linkerd.io/2/tasks/exporting-metrics/

The `linkerd-prometheus` config lives here:
https://github.com/linkerd/linkerd2/blob/65d5778b9393cd41c001b5023cf02a0d7626d03c/charts/linkerd2/templates/prometheus.yaml

All that said, I'd echo 's [recommendation](https://github.com/linkerd/linkerd2/issues/3406#issuecomment-529642159) to leave `linkerd-prometheus` as-is.

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n linkerd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=linkerd
kubectl get events -n linkerd --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Make Prometheus Pluggable" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with linkerd installed or the issue environment reproducible.
